markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's two official delays have now locked GTA VI for a November 19, 2026 console launch, shifting industry focus to the summer marketing push that typically precedes major Rockstar releases. Take-Two's February earnings call confirmed the game remains on track, while recent Best Buy pre-order leaks point to an imminent third trailer that could drop within weeks and further build anticipation. Traders are weighing these confirmed timelines against broader cultural milestones likely to land before launch, including major album cycles and geopolitical developments, creating a fluid market where each new earnings update or marketing leak can quickly reshape implied probabilities across the outcome slate.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive’s repeated official delays have cemented trader consensus that Grand Theft Auto VI will miss any release window before June 2026. After shifting the title from its original 2025 target to May 26, 2026, the studio announced a further postponement in November 2025 to November 19, 2026, citing the need for additional polish on the open-world sequel. This pattern aligns with Rockstar’s history of prioritizing quality over rushed launches, and no credible development updates since then have signaled an acceleration. Traders view the November date as locked, especially ahead of Take-Two’s May 2026 earnings call, which could provide the next formal status update. While an unexpected early certification or surprise drop remains theoretically possible, historical precedents and current production timelines make such an upset highly improbable.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s New York rape retrial has sharply lifted trader confidence in a “No Prison Time” outcome, now implied at 79.3 percent. After three days of deliberations, the jury deadlocked 9-3 in favor of acquittal, prompting the judge to declare a mistrial and giving prosecutors 30 days to decide on a potential fourth trial. Weinstein, already serving a 16-year California sentence from his 2022 conviction that remains under appeal, faces limited upside for additional consecutive time given his age, documented health issues, and the precedent of hung juries in prior proceedings. With no immediate sentencing dates on the horizon and ongoing appeals, the market reflects strong consensus that further incarceration beyond existing terms is unlikely to materialize.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The close implied probabilities between the Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes reflect their comparable regular-season standings, elite goaltending depth, and balanced forward groups that have sustained strong playoff positioning heading into the later stages of the 2025-26 campaign. Both clubs benefit from veteran leadership and favorable matchups in their respective conferences, keeping trader consensus tightly bunched as minor swings in form or injury reports can quickly alter advancement odds. The Vegas Golden Knights hold mid-tier pricing on the strength of their experience but trail due to tougher projected paths, while longer shots like the Montreal Canadiens and Buffalo Sabres remain behind amid questions around consistency and roster construction.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France edges the trader consensus at 17.3% largely because of its unmatched squad depth, Kylian Mbappé’s consistent scoring threat, and heavy recent futures action that has pushed it past Spain. Spain sits just behind at 16.6% on the strength of its European Championship pedigree, tactical cohesion under Luis de la Fuente, and attacking options that include Lamine Yamal when fully fit. England’s 11.3% reflects a balanced midfield and back-to-back major-tournament final appearances, while Brazil and Argentina hover near 9% on raw talent but show less recent consistency in friendlies and qualifiers. The tight cluster among the top six stems from comparable roster quality, favorable group-stage paths for most contenders, and the absence of any single side demonstrating clear dominance heading into the expanded 48-team field.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France edges the trader consensus at 17.3% largely because of its unmatched squad depth, Kylian Mbappé’s consistent scoring threat, and heavy recent futures action that has pushed it past Spain. Spain sits just behind at 16.6% on the strength of its European Championship pedigree, tactical cohesion under Luis de la Fuente, and attacking options that include Lamine Yamal when fully fit. England’s 11.3% reflects a balanced midfield and back-to-back major-tournament final appearances, while Brazil and Argentina hover near 9% on raw talent but show less recent consistency in friendlies and qualifiers. The tight cluster among the top six stems from comparable roster quality, favorable group-stage paths for most contenders, and the absence of any single side demonstrating clear dominance heading into the expanded 48-team field.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2024 election concluded, the contest for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination features a fragmented field without an established frontrunner. Gavin Newsom’s position stems from his record as California governor on issues such as climate legislation and state budgeting, combined with repeated national media exposure. Kamala Harris benefits from prior vice-presidential visibility and party infrastructure ties, while Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support from progressive primary voters focused on economic redistribution and housing policy. Senate and gubernatorial figures like Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro appeal through moderate positioning and performance in swing-state environments. Support is likely to consolidate around candidates who demonstrate strength in 2026 midterm fundraising, secure early endorsements from congressional leadership, and perform well in early-state polling as primary rules and delegate allocation take shape.
